function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_local_search
function.description:
Brave Local Search API provides enrichments for location search results. Access to this API is available only through the Brave Search API Pro plans; confirm the user's plan before using this tool (if the user does not have a Pro plan, use the brave_web_search tool). Searches for local businesses and places using Brave's Local Search API. Best for queries related to physical locations, businesses, restaurants, services, etc.
    
    Returns detailed information including:
        - Business names and addresses
        - Ratings and review counts
        - Phone numbers and opening hours

    Use this when the query implies 'near me', 'in my area', or mentions specific locations (e.g., 'in San Francisco'). This tool automatically falls back to brave_web_search if no local results are found.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: The language of the UI. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-20, default 10). Applies only to web search results (i.e., has no effect on locations, news, videos, etc.)

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filters explicit content (e.g., images and videos), but allows adult domains in search results. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 days. 'pm' - Discovered within the last 31 days. 'py' - Discovered within the last 365 days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. [REDACTED_PHONE]to[REDACTED_PHONE].

text_decorations.description: Whether display strings (e.g. result snippets) should include decoration markers (e.g. highlighting characters).

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_news_search
function.description:
This tool searches for news articles using Brave's News Search API based on the user's query. Use it when you need current news information, breaking news updates, or articles about specific topics, events, or entities.
    
    When to use:
        - Finding recent news articles on specific topics
        - Getting breaking news updates
        - Researching current events or trending stories
        - Gathering news sources and headlines for analysis

    Returns a JSON list of news-related results with title, url, and description. Some results may contain snippets of text from the article.
    
    When relaying results in markdown-supporting environments, always cite sources with hyperlinks.
    
    Examples:
        - "According to [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/technology/china-bans/), China bans uncertified and recalled power banks on planes".
        - "The [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/27/us/technology/ev-sales.html) reports that Tesla's EV sales have increased by 20%".
        - "According to [BBC News](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-65910000), the UK government has announced a new policy to support renewable energy".

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_summarizer
function.description:
Retrieves AI-generated summaries of web search results using Brave's Summarizer API. This tool processes search results to create concise, coherent summaries of information gathered from multiple sources.

    When to use:

    - When you need a concise overview of complex topics from multiple sources
    - For quick fact-checking or getting key points without reading full articles
    - When providing users with summarized information that synthesizes various perspectives
    - For research tasks requiring distilled information from web searches

    Returns a text summary that consolidates information from the search results. Optional features include inline references to source URLs and additional entity information.

    Requirements: Must first perform a web search using brave_web_search with summary=true parameter. Requires a Pro AI subscription to access the summarizer functionality.

key.description: The key is equal to value of field key as part of the Summarizer response model.

entity_info.description: Returns extra entities info with the summary response.

inline_references.description: Adds inline references to the summary response.

function.name: mcp_context7_query_docs
function.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

function.name: mcp_context7_resolve_library_id
function.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.

function.name: [REDACTED_TOKEN]
function.description:
A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
  * Regular analytical steps
  * Revisions of previous thoughts
  * Questions about previous decisions
  * Realizations about needing more analysis
  * Changes in approach
  * Hypothesis generation
  * Hypothesis verification
- nextThoughtNeeded: True if you need more thinking, even if at what seemed like the end
- thoughtNumber: Current number in sequence (can go beyond initial total if needed)
- totalThoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- isRevision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revisesThought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branchFromThought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branchId: Identifier for the current branch (if any)
- needsMoreThoughts: If reaching end but realizing more thoughts needed

You should:
1. Start with an initial estimate of needed thoughts, but be ready to adjust
2. Feel free to question or revise previous thoughts
3. Don't hesitate to add more thoughts if needed, even at the "end"
4. Express uncertainty when present
5. Mark thoughts that revise previous thinking or branch into new paths
6. Ignore information that is irrelevant to the current step
7. Generate a solution hypothesis when appropriate
8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
9. Repeat the process until satisfied with the solution
10. Provide a single, ideally correct answer as the final output
11. Only set nextThoughtNeeded to false when truly done and a satisfactory answer is reached

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thought step is needed

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

{"content": "     1|# Nexa Paraguay — Comparison Pages
     2|**Updated:** April 2026
     3|
     4|> **Core Principle:** Lead with tax numbers. The comparison isn't "Paraguay vs other residency options" — it's "Paraguay's 10% tax vs your current 37-50% tax".
     5|
     6|---
     7|
     8|## Page 1: Paraguay vs. [Your Country]
     9|
    10|### Hero Section
    11|**Headline:** "Stop Paying 40%+ in Taxes. Pay 10% Instead."
    12|
    13|**Subheadline:** "Paraguay's territorial tax system — foreign income not taxed locally — could save you €30,000-50,000 per year."
    14|
    15|---
    16|
    17|### The Numbers (Primary Comparison Table)
    18|
    19|| Factor | Netherlands | Belgium | Germany | **Paraguay** |
    20||--------|-------------|---------|---------|--------------|
    21|| Corporate Tax Rate | 25% | 25% | 15-30% | **10%** |
    22|| Personal Income Tax (top) | 49.5% | 50% | 45% | **10%** (IRE) |
    23|| Tax System | Worldwide | Worldwide | Worldwide | **Territorial** |
    24|| Foreign Income Taxed | Yes | Yes | Yes | **No** |
    25|| Tax on Dividends | 15% | 25% + krank | 25% | **8%** |
    26|| Wealth Tax | Box 3 | No | No | **No** |
    27|| Exit Tax | Yes | Yes | Yes | **Minimal** |
    28|| Investment Required | None | None | None | **None** |
    29|| Days Required in Country | 183+ | 183+ | 183+ | **0** |
    30|| Path to Citizenship | 5 years | 5 years | 5-8 years | **3 years** |
    31|
    32|**Bottom line:** Paraguay costs less and taxes less.
    33|
    34|---
    35|
    36|### Key Differentiators
    37|
    38|#### 1. "Territorial" vs "Worldwide" — What It Means
    39|
    40|**Your current system (NL/BE/DE):**
    41|- You pay tax on income wherever it's generated
    42|- Even income earned outside your home country gets taxed at your home country rates
    43|- Foreign investments, dividends, rental income — all taxed
    44|
    45|**Paraguay's territorial system:**
    46|- Only income *generated within Paraguay* is taxed
    47|- Foreign income (dividends, royalties, consulting fees, rental income) = 0% tax
    48|- This is legal and common in LATAM; Paraguay isn't on the EU blacklist
    49|
    50|#### 2. The Investment Question
    51|
    52|| Country | Investment Required |
    53||---------|-------------------|
    54|| Portugal | €500,000 (real estate) |
    55|| Spain | €500,000 (real estate) |
    56|| Greece | €250,000 (real estate) |
    57|| Malta | €600,000+ |
    58|| UK | £2,000,000 |
    59|| **Paraguay** | **None** |
    60|
    61|You don't need to buy property to get residency. Your capital stays liquid.
    62|
    63|#### 3. The Days Question
    64|
    65|| Country | Physical Presence Required |
    66||---------|--------------------------|
    67|| Portugal | 183 days/year |
    68|| Spain | 183 days/year |
    69|| Netherlands | 183 days/year |
    70|| Germany | 183 days/year |
    71|| **Paraguay** | **0 days/year** (after initial process) |
    72|
    73|Once residency is approved, you only need to visit every 3 years to maintain it.
    74|
    75|---
    76|
    77|### Who This Is For
    78|
    79|**This works if:**
    80|- You earn over €60,000/year in active income
    81|- You have passive income (dividends, rental, royalties) you want to structure
    82|- You want to reduce your tax burden without renouncing citizenship
    83|- You're open to relocating or splitting your time
    84|
    85|**This may not be for you if:**
    86|- Your income is below €40,000/year (the savings may not justify the move)
    87|- You need to stay in the EU for career reasons
    88|- You have young children in school (adjustment period)
    89|
    90|---
    91|
    92|## Page 2: Paraguay vs. Other Residency Destinations
    93|
    94|### Comparison: Tax-Focused Residency Programs
    95|
    96|| Factor | Paraguay | Portugal (ex-NHR) | Spain | Greece | Malta | Dubai |
    97||--------|----------|-------------------|-------|--------|-------|-------|
    98|| Corporate Tax | 10% | 20-48% | 25% | 24% | 35% | 0-9% |
    99|| Personal Tax | 10% | 20-48% | 47% | 44% | 35% | 0% |
   100|| Foreign Income | 0% | Taxed | Taxed | Taxed | Taxed | 0% |
   101|| Investment | None | €500K | €500K | €250K | €600K+ | $2M+ |
   102|| EU Access | No | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | No |
   103|| Banking | Good | Good | Good | Good | Good | Excellent |
   104|| Residency Path | 3 years | 5 years | 10 years | 7 years | 1-3 years | 0 years |
   105|
   106|**Why We Win on Tax:**
   107|- Portugal's NHR is dead. Current rates: 20-48%.
   108|- Spain requires €500K investment + 183 days + worldwide taxation.
   109|- Greece has improved but still 24% corporate + 44% personal.
   110|- Malta has high taxes despite "tax residency" programs.
   111|- Dubai has no personal income tax but requires $2M+ investment + complex structures.
   112|
   113|**Paraguay:** No investment, no days, 10% tax, territorial system.
   114|
   115|---
   116|
   117|### Comparison: Tax Haven Alternatives
   118|
   119|| Factor | Paraguay | Panama | Costa Rica | Uruguay | Caribbean |
   120||--------|----------|--------|------------|---------|-----------|
   121|| Corporate Tax | 10% | 10-20% | 10-25% | 25% | 0-15% |
   122|| EU Blacklist | No | No | No | No | Some |
   123|| Banking Access | Good | Good | Good | Good | Declining |
   124|| US Embassy | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Mixed |
   125|| Flight Access (AMS) | 11-12hrs | 10hrs | 10hrs | 13hrs | 8-10hrs |
   126|
   127|**Why Not Caribbean:**
   128|- EU has blacklisted several Caribbean programs
   129|- Banking is increasingly difficult (AML concerns)
   130|- USvisa issues for some programs
   131|- Perception problems with home-country tax authorities
   132|
   133|**Paraguay:** Clean, not blacklisted, reliable banking, professional infrastructure.
   134|
   135|---
   136|
   137|## Page 3: The Exit Tax Question (NL/BE Specific)
   138|
   139|### Dutch Exit Tax — What You Need to Know
   140|
   141|| Factor | Without Planning | With Proper Planning |
   142||--------|------------------|---------------------|
   143|| Exit Tax Rate | 25% on capital gains | Can be deferred |
   144|| Timing | Immediate at departure | 5-10 year deferral |
   145|| Shareholdings | Full taxation | Often deferred |
   146|| Box 3 | Continues until exit | Ends at exit |
   147|
   148|**How We Help:**
   149|1. Coordinate with Dutch tax counsel before departure
   150|2. Structure exit to minimize immediate exit tax
   151|3. Time the exit to avoid double-taxation years
   152|4. Document everything for Dutch tax authority
   153|
   154|**The Key Insight:**
   155|Exit tax isn't avoided — it's deferred. If you're leaving NL for Paraguay, the tax authority expects you to pay eventually. The question is: can you defer it long enough that the present value is significantly lower? Answer: often yes.
   156|
   157|---
   158|
   159|### Belgian Exit Tax — Similar Framework
   160|
   161|| Factor | Belgium |
   162||--------|---------|
   163|| Exit Tax | Capital gains taxed at progressive rates |
   164|| Timing | At departure |
   165|| Deferred Exit | Possible with proper structure |
   166|
   167|**We work with Belgian tax specialists** who understand the Belgian exit tax rules and can structure your departure to minimize the hit.
   168|
   169|---
   170|
   171|## Page 4: Paraguay vs. Portugal (The NHR Comparison)
   172|
   173|### Why Portugal's NHR Is No Longer Relevant
   174|
   175|**Timeline:**
   176|- 2020: NHR introduced — 20% flat rate on Portuguese income
   177|- 2023: Government signals NHR changes
   178|- 2024: NHR essentially dead — rates now 20-48%
   179|- 2025: Full repeal discussions
   180|
   181|**Current Portugal Reality:**
   182|| Factor | Portugal (Current) | Paraguay |
   183||--------|-------------------|----------|
   184|| Corporate Tax | 20-31.5% | 10% |
   185|| Personal Tax | 20-48% | 10% |
   186|| Foreign Income | Now taxed | Not taxed |
   187|| Investment Required | €500K | None |
   188|| Days Required | 183/year | 0/year |
   189|
   190|**Bottom Line:** If you were counting on Portugal's NHR, it's time to reconsider. Paraguay offers better rates, no investment, and no days requirement.
   191|
   192|---
   193|
   194|## Page 5: Our Process vs. Competitors
   195|
   196|### Why Nexa Paraguay Wins
   197|
   198|| Factor | Nexa Paraguay | Typical Competitor |
   199||--------|---------------|-------------------|
   200|| Price | €2,900-6,900 (fixed) | €3,000-10,000+ |
   201|| Trips Required | 1 (2-3 days) | 1-3 |
   202|| Team | Lawyers + Accountants + Notaries + Bank | Often single point of contact |
   203|| Timeline | 8-12 weeks | 12-24 weeks |
   204|| Banking | We introduce you | You're on your own |
   205|| Fixed Price | Yes | Often "plus expenses" |
   206|| Languages | EN/ES/NL/DE | Usually only ES/EN |
   207|
   208|### What "Full Service" Actually Means
   209|
   210|1. **Legal:** Immigration lawyers handle all Migraciones paperwork
   211|2. **Accounting:** CPAs set up your Paraguayan company (if applicable) and tax registration
   212|3. **Notary:** Public notary (escribano) for all legal documents
   213|4. **Banking:** We have relationships with Banco do Brasil, Banco Continental, and Itau — we introduce you
   214|5. **Advisory:** 12 months of legal and tax advisory included
   215|
   216|**What competitors often don't include:**
   217|- Bank introduction (this is the hardest part)
   218|- Company setup beyond basic residency
   219|- Ongoing advisory
   220|- Coordination with your home-country advisors

{"content": "     1|# Nexa Paraguay — FAQ: Top 15 Dealclosing Questions
     2|**Updated:** April 2026
     3|
     4|> **Principle:** These are the questions that actually close deals. Not generic FAQs — questions prospects ask when they're seriously considering Paraguay. Each answer connects to the 10% tax story.
     5|
     6|---
     7|
     8|## Tax Questions (The Core)
     9|
    10|### Q1: "Is the 10% tax rate guaranteed?"
    11|
    12|**Short Answer:** Yes, it's the law (Ley 6380, since 2020).
    13|
    14|**Full Answer:**
    15|The 10% IRE (Impuesto a la Renta Empresarial) is the current corporate income tax rate under Paraguayan law (Ley 6380, effective since 2020). This isn't a "special rate" or "loophole" — it's the standard corporate rate. The territorial system (no tax on foreign income) has been policy since 2014. Both have remained stable through multiple administrations. While no tax law is permanent, the stability record is strong, and there's no current political movement to change it. We structure your setup to remain compliant under whatever rules apply.
    16|
    17|---
    18|
    19|### Q2: "Will my home country still tax me?"
    20|
    21|**Short Answer:** Once you properly relocate, typically no.
    22|
    23|**Full Answer:**
    24|This depends on your tax residency. Once you formally emigrate and cut ties (for Netherlands: woonplaatsverklaring; for Belgium: tax emigration declaration; for Germany: Abmeldung), your home country generally loses the right to tax your worldwide income. Paraguay's territorial system then applies: only income generated *within* Paraguay is taxed at 10%. Your foreign income (dividends, royalties, rental income, consulting fees from non-Paraguayan sources) is taxed at 0%. We coordinate with your home-country tax advisors to ensure the transition is clean.
    25|
    26|---
    27|
    28|### Q3: "What about the Dutch exit tax / box 3?"
    29|
    30|**Short Answer:** It can often be deferred, not avoided.
    31|
    32|**Full Answer:**
    33|Dutch exit tax (exitheffing) on "aanmerkelijk belang" (substantial interest, typically >5% shareholding) is triggered when you renounce Dutch tax residency. However, it can often be **deferred** rather than paid immediately — especially if you relocate to another EU/EEA country (Paraguay is not in the EEA, but there are structuring options). Box 3 ends once you formally exit — but the key is timing. We coordinate with Dutch tax counsel to structure your exit so you don't get double-taxed in a transition year. You should involve your Dutch accountant from the start. We can connect you with specialists.
    34|
    35|---
    36|
    37|### Q4: "How does this compare to Portugal's old NHR?"
    38|
    39|**Short Answer:** Paraguay is better now. Portugal's NHR is dead.
    40|
    41|**Full Answer:**
    42|Portugal's Non-Habitual Resident (NHR) program — which offered 20% flat tax — is essentially dead as of [REDACTED_PHONE]. Current rates are 20-48%. Paraguay offers 10% IRE + territorial system (no tax on foreign income) + no investment requirement + no days requirement. The math is simple: for income over €60K/year, Paraguay wins. If you were counting on Portugal, it's time to pivot.
    43|
    44|---
    45|
    46|### Q5: "Is Paraguay on the EU tax blacklist?"
    47|
    48|**Short Answer:** No. It's not blacklisted.
    49|
    50|**Full Answer:**
    51|Paraguay is **not** on the EU's list of non-cooperative tax jurisdictions (the "blacklist"). It has exchange of information agreements and participates in CRS (Common Reporting Standard). This means: legitimate tax planning, not evasion. Your home-country tax authority will know about your Paraguayan account (just as they'd know about any foreign account), but that doesn't trigger taxation — your tax residency does.
    52|
    53|---
    54|
    55|## Process Questions
    56|
    57|### Q6: "How long does the process take?"
    58|
    59|**Short Answer:** 8-12 weeks from document submission.
    60|
    61|**Full Answer:**
    62|- Weeks 1-2: Document preparation (from your home country)
    63|- Week 3: We file everything in Asunción
    64|- Weeks 4-8: Migraciones processing
    65|- Week 9: Approval + 2-day trip for fingerprints/biometrics
    66|- Weeks 10-12: Cédula (ID card) issued, full setup complete
    67|
    68|You only need to travel once, for 2-3 days. Everything else is handled remotely.
    69|
    70|---
    71|
    72|### Q7: "How many trips do I need?"
    73|
    74|**Short Answer:** One. Two if you want to include a property tour.
    75|
    76|**Full Answer:**
    77|Just one trip is required, typically 2-3 days in Asunción. We coordinate everything: airport pickup, hotel, Migraciones appointment, and bank account opening (yes, you get the bank account during this trip). If you're interested in property investment, we can arrange a property tour as part of the trip or separately.
    78|
    79|---
    80|
    81|### Q8: "What documents do I need?"
    82|
    83|**Full Answer:**
    84|- Passport (certified copy)
    85|- Birth certificate (apostilled)
    86|- Marriage certificate (if applicable, apostilled)
    87|- Proof of income / bank statements (3 months)
    88|- Criminal background check from your home country (apostilled)
    89|
    90|Our team handles: power of attorney, company incorporation (if Business program), all submissions. We walk you through the document process step by step.
    91|
    92|---
    93|
    94|### Q9: "What if the application is rejected?"
    95|
    96|**Full Answer:**
    97|Rejections are extremely rare — our 97% completion rate reflects careful pre-screening. Most rejections happen due to: criminal record issues, incomplete documentation, or passport validity problems. We vet thoroughly before submission. If there's an issue, we work with you to resolve it. In the unlikely event of rejection, we'd discuss your options, which may include addressing the issue and reapplying.
    98|
    99|---
   100|
   101|## Financial Questions
   102|
   103|### Q10: "How much does it cost?"
   104|
   105|**Short Answer:** €2,900-6,900 depending on program.
   106|
   107|**Full Answer:**
   108|- **Base Program:** €2,900 — Temporary residency + document preparation + process coordination
   109|- **Business Program:** €4,400+ — Above + company setup + RUC (tax ID) + bank introduction + first-year accounting
   110|- **Investor Program:** €6,900+ — Above + SUACE (investor residency) + permanent residency path + extended advisory
   111|
   112|All prices are fixed and include: legal fees, notary fees, government taxes, bank coordination, and 12 months of legal/tax advisory. No surprise "extras."
   113|
   114|---
   115|
   116|### Q11: "What about banking? I've heard it's difficult."
   117|
   118|**Full Answer:**
   119|Banking is the hardest part of any Paraguayan relocation — and where most DIY attempts fail. We have relationships with Banco do Brasil, Banco Continental, and Itau. We **introduce you** to the bank, provide supporting documentation, and in most cases, accounts are opened during your 2-day trip. It's not guaranteed (the bank makes the final decision), but our success rate is high because we know exactly what they want.
   120|
   121|---
   122|
   123|### Q12: "Can I keep my current bank account in NL/BE/DE?"
   124|
   125|**Full Answer:**
   126|This depends on the bank. Some Dutch banks (ING, ABN) have closed accounts for residents who relocate to non-EU countries. Others (Rabobank, some regional banks) are more flexible. Our advice: keep your home-country bank account open for 6-12 months after relocation to ensure clean tax transition. After that, you can likely close it. We recommend opening your Paraguayan account first, then gradually transitioning.
   127|
   128|---
   129|
   130|## Lifestyle Questions
   131|
   132|### Q13: "Is it safe?"
   133|
   134|**Full Answer:**
   135|Asunción is safer than many assume. The expat neighborhoods (Manorá, Los Laureles, gated communities near Shopping del Sol) are comparable to European suburban crime rates. Clients walk, go to restaurants, send kids to schools — without issues. Like any city, there are areas to avoid. We provide neighborhood guidance as part of the onboarding. Overall: 250+ clients have relocated successfully, and we'd know if there were systemic safety issues.
   136|
   137|---
   138|
   139|### Q14: "What about schools for my kids?"
   140|
   141|**Full Answer:**
   142|Options include:
   143|- **American School of Asunción** — Full English curriculum, US-accredited
   144|- **Colegium** — Bilingual ES/EN, IB program
   145|- **British School** — British curriculum
   146|- **Various bilingual schools** — ES/EN or ES/PT
   147|
   148|For Dutch/Belgian families: there isn't a dedicated European school, but the bilingual options work well. Many families say the quality of life (safer, more outdoor time, lower cost) outweighs the adjustment. We'd connect you with clients who have kids in these schools.
   149|
   150|---
   151|
   152|### Q15: "Can I work remotely for my current employer?"
   153|
   154|**Full Answer:**
   155|Yes, with caveats. As a resident, you can work for any employer (Paraguayan or foreign) or be self-employed. However, if you're working remotely for a Dutch/Belgian/German employer while physically in Paraguay, there may be payroll tax implications for your employer. Most clients on "remote work" arrangements either:
   156|1. Have their employer set up a local payroll in Paraguay (we can advise)
   157|2. Structure as self-employed / freelance
   158|3. Are already business owners who invoice from their Paraguayan company
   159|
   160|The specifics depend on your employment situation. Let's discuss during your consultation.
   161|
   162|---
   163|
   164|## CTA: Have a Different Question?
   165|
   166|> **"Don't see your question here? Book a call and I'll answer it directly."**
   167|
   168|[Link: Book Free Consultation]
   169|
   170|---
   171|
   172|*Document Version: 1.0 — Updated April 2026*
   173|*For Nexa Paraguay website FAQ section*

   276|Context: Post-call follow-up
   277|Track: testimonial_email_include
   278|```

{"content": "     1|# Nexa Paraguay — WhatsApp Integration
     2|**Updated:** April 2026
     3|
     4|> **Goal:** Convert website visitors via WhatsApp. Low effort, high conversion.
     5|
     6|---
     7|
     8|## 1. WhatsApp Business Setup
     9|
    10|### Account Configuration
    11|
    12|| Setting | Value |
    13||---------|-------|
    14|| Business Name | Nexa Paraguay |
    15|| Phone | +595 [see site content] |
    16|| Category | Legal Services |
    17|| Timezone | PY (UTC-3) |
    18|| Language | ES primary, EN/NL/DE secondary |
    19|
    20|### Profile
    21|
    22|```
    23|About: Residency & tax solutions in Paraguay. 10% IRE. 250+ clients.
    24|Address: Asunción, Paraguay
    25|Website: https://nexaparaguay.com
    26|```
    27|
    28|---
    29|
    30|## 2. Quick Replies (Pre-set Responses)
    31|
    32|### Greeting (Automated)
    33|
    34|```
    35|¡Hola! 👋
    36|
    37|Gracias por contactarnos. Soy [Name] de Nexa Paraguay.
    38|
    39|¿En qué puedo ayudarte hoy?
    40|
    41|💬 Puedo responder preguntas sobre:
    42|• Residency en Paraguay
    43|• Optimización fiscal (10% IRE)
    44|• Programas disponibles
    45|• Proceso y timeline
    46|
    47|📅 También puedes agendar una consulta gratuita aquí:
    48|[Link to booking]
    49|
    50|¿Te escribe en español, inglés, holandés o alemán?
    51|```
    52|
    53|### Initial Greeting (After Hours)
    54|
    55|```
    56|¡Hola! 👋
    57|
    58|Gracias por tu mensaje. Nuestro equipo opera de lunes a viernes, 9:00-18:00 (hora Paraguay).
    59|

    61|
    62|📞 Para consultas urgentes:
    63|• Tel: +595 [phone]
    64|• Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL]
    65|
    66|Si ya conoces tu situación, puedes agendar aquí:
    67|[Link to booking]
    68|
    69|¡Gracias por tu interés en Paraguay!
    70|```
    71|
    72|### Qualification Questions
    73|
    74|```
    75|¡Perfecto! Para darte la mejor información, necesito conocer un poco tu situación:
    76|
    77|1. ¿Cuál es tu país de residencia actual?
    78|2. ¿Cuál es tu ingreso anual aproximado?
    79|3. ¿Cuál es tu objetivo principal? (residencia fiscal, optimización de impuestos, negocio)
    80|4. ¿Cuál es tu timeline? (pronto, este año, solo explorando)
    81|
    82|También te invito a usar nuestra calculadora de ahorro fiscal:
    83|[Link to calculator]
    84|
    85|Mientras tanto, responderé tus preguntas. 😊
    86|```
    87|
    88|### Program Explanation
    89|
    90|```
    91|Nuestros programas:
    92|
    93|📋 BASE — €2,900
    94|   Residencia temporal + coordinación
    95|
    96|💼 NEGOCIOS — €4,400+
    97|   Arriba + empresa + RUC + cuenta bancaria
    98|
    99|🚀 INVERSOR — €6,900+
   100|   Arriba + SUACE + residencia permanente
   101|
   102|Todos incluyen:
   103|✓ 8-12 semanas timeline
   104|✓ Un solo viaje
   105|✓ Precio fijo (sin sorpresas)
   106|✓ Equipo completo (abogados, contadores, notarios)
   107|
   108|¿Cuál te interesa más? ¿O quieres que te ayude a elegir?
   109|```
   110|
   111|### Booking Confirmation
   112|
   113|```
   114|¡Excelente! 👇
   115|
   116|Para confirmar tu consulta gratuita de 30 minutos:
   117|
   118|📅 Agenda aquí:
   119|[Calendly link]
   120|
   121|Antes de la llamada, te enviamos un breve formulario para preparar temas.
   122|
   123|¿Tienes alguna pregunta específica que quieras cubrir en la llamada?
   124|```
   125|
   126|### Calculator Follow-up
   127|
   128|```
   129|¡Hola! 👋
   130|
   131|Vimos que usaste nuestra calculadora de ahorro fiscal. ¡Buenas noticias!
   132|
   133|Según los datos que ingresaste, podrías ahorrar aproximadamente [X]€ al año con Paraguay.
   134|
   135|💡 Esto es porque Paraguay tiene:
   136|• 10% IRE (impuesto corporativo)
   137|• Sistema territorial (ingresos extranjeros no gravan)
   138|• No requiere inversión para residencia
   139|
   140|¿Quieres hablar con un asesor sobre tu situación específica?
   141|📅 Agenda aquí: [Calendly link]
   142|
   143|O responde tus preguntas aquí. 😊
   144|```
   145|
   146|### Post-Consultation Follow-up
   147|
   148|```
   149|¡Hola! 👋
   150|
   151|Gracias por nuestra llamada de hoy.
   152|
   153|Resumen de lo que discutimos:
   154|• Tu programa recomendado: [X]
   155|• Inversión estimada: €[X]
   156|• Timeline: [X] semanas
   157|
   158|Próximos pasos:
   159|1. Revisa los detalles del programa: [Link]
   160|2. Si tienes preguntas, responde este mensaje
   161|3. Si estás listo,firmamos el acuerdo y comenzamos
   162|
   163|¿Alguna pregunta mientras lo reflexionas?
   164|```
   165|
   166|### Final Nudge (No Response)
   167|
   168|```
   169|¡Hola! 👋
   170|
   171|Solo pasaba a recordar que estamos aquí cuando estés listo/a.
   172|
   173|Resolver Paraguay tu situación fiscal no es una decisión pequeña, pero los números son claros:
   174|• 10% vs 40-50% en impuestos
   175|• Sistema territorial
   176|• Residency en 8-12 semanas
   177|
   178|📅 Agenda tu consulta cuando quieras:
   179|[Calendly link]
   180|
   181|¡Saludos!
   182|[Nexa Paraguay]
   183|```
   184|
   185|---
   186|
   187|## 3. Click-to-Chat Button
   188|
   189|### Implementation Status: ✅ DONE
   190|
   191|The WhatsApp floating button is already implemented at:
   192|```
   193|web/components/sections/whatsapp-float.tsx
   194|```
   195|
   196|**Features:**
   197|- ✅ Multi-language support (ES/EN/NL/DE/PT)
   198|- ✅ Context-aware messages (home, programas, proceso, calculadora, contacto)
   199|- ✅ Analytics tracking (`whatsapp_cta_click` event)
   200|- ✅ Configured on all major pages via `pages/*.json`
   201|- ✅ Phone number in `site.json`: `[REDACTED_PHONE]`
   202|
   203|**Usage:**
   204|```tsx
   205|<WhatsAppFloat 
   206|  phone="[REDACTED_PHONE]"
   207|  __locale="es"
   208|  __context="home" // auto-selects contextual message
   209|  __track={true}
   210|/>
   211|```
   212|
   213|**Context Messages:**
   214|| Page Context | Message |
   215||-------------|---------|
   216|| home | Calculator follow-up |
   217|| programas | Program info request |
   218|| proceso | Process questions |
   219|| calculadora | Post-calculator inquiry |
   220|| contacto | Booking request |
   221|
   222|### CTA Options (Pre-filled Message)
   223|
   224|| Context | Pre-filled Message |
   225||---------|-------------------|
   226|| General | "Hola! Estoy interesado en información sobre Paraguay" |
   227|| Calculator | "Hola! Acabo de usar la calculadora y quiero saber más" |
   228|| Specific program | "Hola! Quiero información del programa [BASE/NEGOCIOS/INVERSOR]" |
   229|| Tax question | "Hola! Tengo una pregunta específica sobre impuestos" |
   230|| Callback | "Hola! Prefiero que me llamen" |
   231|
   232|---
   233|
   234|## 4. Labels & Pipeline
   235|
   236|### WhatsApp Business Labels
   237|
   238|| Label | Color | Use |
   239||-------|-------|-----|
   240|| 🔴 Hot | Red | Ready to book / high intent |
   241|| 🟡 Warm | Yellow | In conversation / nurturing |
   242|| 🔵 Cold | Blue | New lead / not responded |
   243|| ✅ Closed | Green | Converted to client |
   244|| 📅 Booked | Purple | Has consultation booked |
   245|| ❌ Not Interested | Gray | Explicit no |
   246|
   247|### Automation Rules
   248|
   249|```
   250|1. New contact → Label "Cold"
   251|2. Replied within 1 hour → Label "Warm"  
   252|3. Booked consultation → Label "Booked"
   253|4. Closed to client → Label "Closed" + move to CRM
   254|5. No response in 48h → Add to email nurture sequence
   255|6. "Stop" / "No thanks" → Label "Not Interested" + suppress
   256|```
   257|
   258|---
   259|
   260|## 5. Analytics Events
   261|
   262|### Track
   263|
   264|| Event | When |
   265||-------|------|
   266|| `whatsapp_message_received` | Incoming message |
   267|| `whatsapp_quick_reply_sent` | Quick reply used |
   268|| `whatsapp_cta_clicked` | Clicked Calendly link |
   269|| `whatsapp_booked` | Consultation booked via WhatsApp |
   270|| `whatsapp_response_time` | Time from lead to first response |
   271|
   272|### Response Time Targets
   273|
   274|| Lead Type | Target Response |
   275||-----------|----------------|
   276|| Hot (booked call) | Within 15 minutes |
   277|| Warm | Within 1 hour |
   278|| Cold | Within 4 hours |
   279|
   280|---
   281|
   282|## 6. Multi-Language Support
   283|
   284|### Language Detection
   285|
   286|```
   287|If message contains NL keywords → Reply in Dutch
   288|If message contains DE keywords → Reply in German  
   289|If message contains EN keywords → Reply in English
   290|Else → Reply in Spanish
   291|```
   292|
   293|### NL Keywords
   294|```
   295|belasting, belastingen, Niederlande, Nederlands, emigreren, exitbelasting, box 3
   296|```
   297|
   298|### DE Keywords
   299|```
   300|Steuer, Deutschland, emigration, Steuern,exitsteuer, deutsche
   301|```
   302|
   303|### EN Keywords
   304|```
   305|tax, residency, moving, Netherlands, Belgium, Germany, process
   306|```
   307|
   308|---
   309|
   310|## 7. Away Messages
   311|
   312|### Business Hours
   313|
   314|```
   315|Mon-Fri: 9:00-18:00 PY (UTC-3)
   316|```
   317|
   318|### Away Message (Weekend)
   319|
   320|```
   321|¡Hola! 👋
   322|
   323|Gracias por tu mensaje. Es fin de semana y nuestro equipo descansa (como todo el mundo 😄).
   324|
   325|Respondemos el lunes a primera hora.
   326|
   327|💡 Mientras tanto:
   328|• Mira nuestra calculadora: [Link]
   329|• Lee sobre nuestros programas: [Link]
   330|• Preguntas frecuentes: [Link]
   331|
   332|¡Que tengas un buen fin de semana! 👋
   333|```
   334|
   335|### Away Message (Night)
   336|
   337|```
   338|¡Hola! 👋
   339|
   340|Gracias por tu mensaje. Ya es de noche en Paraguay (son las [TIME] hrs).
   341|
   342|Nuestro equipo responde de lunes a viernes, 9:00-18:00 PY.
   343|
   344|Si tienes prisa, escríbenos un email: [REDACTED_EMAIL]
   345|
   346|¡Hasta pronto! 👋
   347|```
   348|
   349|---
   350|
   351|## 8. Integration with CRM
   352|
   353|### Flow
   354|
   355|```
   356|WhatsApp → Webhook → Supabase
   357|           ↓
   358|     Lead created/updated
   359|           ↓
   360|     Tag: whatsapp_lead
   361|           ↓
   362|     Email sequence triggered
   363|```
   364|
   365|### Supabase Schema
   366|
   367|```sql
   368|CREATE TABLE whatsapp_conversations (
   369|  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
   370|  phone VARCHAR(20),
   371|  lead_id UUID REFERENCES leads(id),
   372|  last_message TEXT,
   373|  last_message_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   374|  status 'active' | 'closed',
   375|  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   376|  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   377|);
   378|
   379|CREATE TABLE whatsapp_messages (
   380|  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
   381|  conversation_id UUID,
   382|  direction 'inbound' | 'outbound',
   383|  content TEXT,
   384|  quick_reply_used BOOLEAN,
   385|  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   386|);
   387|```
   388|
   389|---
   390|
   391|## 9. Campaign Tracking
   392|
   393|### UTM Parameters
   394|
   395|```
   396|Source: whatsapp
   397|Medium: chat
   398|Campaign: [specific_campaign]
   399|Content: [cta_button_variant]
   400|```
   401|
   402|### Attribution
   403|
   404|```
   405|Lead source = whatsapp
   406|  → Attribution: WhatsApp
   407|  → Revenue: attributed to WhatsApp conversions
   408|```
   409|
   410|---
   411|
   412|*Document Version: 1.0 — Updated April 2026*
   413|*For Nexa Paraguay marketing team*
   414|", "total_lines": 413, "file_size": 8932, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Nexa Paraguay — Integration Setup Guide
     2|## For Kiki Weiss / Nexa team
     3|
     4|This guide explains what accounts you need to create and what information to send us so we can connect everything.
     5|
     6|---
     7|
     8|## 1. Google Analytics (GA4) — Required
     9|
    10|**What it does:** Tracks website visitors, page views, conversions, and ad performance.
    11|
    12|### Step-by-step
    13|
    14|1. Go to https://analytics.google.com
    15|2. Sign in with your Google account (or create one)
    16|3. Click **Start measuring** → Create a new property
    17|4. Property name: **Nexa Paraguay Website**
    18|5. Reporting time zone: **America/Asuncion**
    19|6. Currency: **USD**
    20|7. Click **Create**
    21|8. Select **Web** as the platform
    22|9. Website URL: **https://nexaparaguay.com**
    23|10. Click **Create stream**
    24|11. Copy the **Measurement ID** (looks like `G-XXXXXXXXXX`)
    25|
    26|### Send us
    27|
    28|```
    29|Measurement ID: G-____________
    30|```
    31|
    32|---
    33|
    34|## 2. HubSpot CRM — Required
    35|
    36|**What it does:** Captures contact form submissions, stores leads, sends automated emails.
    37|
    38|### Step-by-step
    39|
    40|1. Go to https://www.hubspot.com/products/crm
    41|2. Sign up for a **Free CRM** account
    42|3. After setup, go to your HubSpot dashboard
    43|4. Click the gear icon (Settings) → **Tracking & Analytics** → **Tracking Code**
    44|5. Copy your **Hub ID** (a number like `[REDACTED_PHONE]`)
    45|
    46|### Create a Contact Form
    47|
    48|1. In HubSpot, go to **Marketing** → **Lead Capture** → **Forms**
    49|2. Click **Create form**
    50|3. Choose **Embedded form**
    51|4. Add these fields:
    52|   - First name (required)
    53|   - Last name (required)
    54|   - Email (required)
    55|   - Phone (optional)
    56|   - Country of residence (dropdown: Netherlands, Germany, Spain, Belgium, Other)
    57|   - Program interest (dropdown: Base, Business, Investor, Land, Not sure)
    58|   - Message (textarea, optional)
    59|5. Click **Update options** → set submission action to **"Show thank you message"**
    60|6. Publish the form
    61|7. Copy the **Form ID** from the URL or embed code (looks like `a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef[REDACTED_PHONE]`)
    62|
    63|### Send us
    64|
    65|```
    66|HubSpot Portal ID: _______________
    67|Contact Form ID:   _______________
    68|HubSpot account email: ___________
    69|```
    70|
    71|---
    72|
    73|## 3. Mailchimp — Recommended
    74|
    75|**What it does:** Manages email newsletter subscribers and sends email campaigns.
    76|
    77|### Step-by-step
    78|
    79|1. Go to https://mailchimp.com
    80|2. Create a free account
    81|3. Once logged in, go to **Audience** → **Manage Audience** → **Settings**
    82|4. Find your **Audience ID** (in the URL or under "Audience name and defaults")
    83|
    84|### Create a Signup Form (optional but recommended)
    85|
    86|1. Go to **Audience** → **Signup forms**
    87|2. Choose **Embedded form**
    88|3. Customize fields (Name, Email)
    89|4. Copy the form action URL
    90|
    91|### Send us
    92|
    93|```
    94|Mailchimp Audience ID:  audience-_________
    95|Mailchimp API Key:      ___________
    96|```
    97|
    98|---
    99|
   100|## 4. Calendly — Needs setup
   101|
   102|**Status: ❌ Link returns 404**
   103|
   104|Current booking link (returns 404): `https://calendly.com/nexaparaguay/consulta`
   105|
   106|### Step-by-step
   107|
   108|1. Go to https://calendly.com and click **Get started** (free plan works)
   109|2. Create an account with your Nexa Paraguay email
   110|3. Once logged in, go to **Event Types** → **New event type**
   111|4. Choose **One-on-one**
   112|5. Name: **Free Consultation** (or "Consulta Gratuita")
   113|6. Location: **Phone Call** or **Zoom / Google Meet** (your choice)
   114|7. Duration: **30 minutes**
   115|8. Set your availability (business hours, timezone America/Asuncion)
   116|9. Click **Continue** → **Save and close**
   117|10. Copy the booking link — it looks like `https://calendly.com/nexaparaguay/30min` or similar
   118|
   119|### Send us
   120|
   121|```
   122|Calendly booking URL: https://calendly.com/_________________
   123|Calendly account email: _________________

{"path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/06-marketing/comparisons.md", "content": "# Comparisons — Competitor & Country Pages

**Purpose:** Comparison tables used on the website to contrast Paraguay's tax and
residency offering against European home countries (NL/BE/DE) and alternative
residency destinations (Portugal, Spain, Greece, Malta, Dubai, Panama, etc.)

**Last updated:** 2026-04

**Cross-references:** `faq-dealclosing.md`, `lead-magnets.md`,
`/root/nexa-paraguay/marketing/comparisons.md` (source)

---

## Core Strategy

Lead with tax numbers. The comparison is not "Paraguay vs other residency
options" — it is "Paraguay's 10% tax vs your current 37-50% tax."

## Comparison Pages Overview

### Page 1: Paraguay vs. Your Country (NL/BE/DE)

Headline: "Stop Paying 40%+ in Taxes. Pay 10% Instead."

- **Corporate Tax:** Paraguay 10% vs NL 25% / BE 25% / DE 15-30%
- **Personal Income (top):** Paraguay 10% vs NL 49.5% / BE 50% / DE 45%
- **System:** Territorial (Paraguay) vs Worldwide (all three)
- **Foreign Income:** Not taxed in Paraguay; fully taxed in NL/BE/DE
- **Dividends:** 8% vs 15-25%
- **Days Required:** 0/year (Paraguay) vs 183+/year
- **Citizenship:** 3 years (Paraguay) vs 5-8 years

### Page 2: Paraguay vs Other Residency Destinations

Compared against: Portugal (ex-NHR), Spain, Greece, Malta, Dubai, Panama,
Costa Rica, Uruguay, Caribbean programs.

Key differentiators:
- **No investment required** (vs 250K-2M+ for most programs)
- **No physical days requirement** after initial approval
- **Not on EU tax blacklist** (unlike several Caribbean programs)
- **Stable banking** with Banco do Brasil, Continental, Itau

### Page 3: Exit Tax (NL/BE Specific)

- Dutch exit tax (25% on substantial-interest gains) can often be **deferred**
  with proper structuring, not avoided
- Belgian exit tax follows similar principles with progressive rates
- Coordination with home-country tax counsel is essential

### Page 4: Paraguay vs Portugal (ex-NHR)

Portugal's NHR is dead ([REDACTED_PHONE]). Current rates 20-48%. Paraguay wins on
rate, investment, and days requirement.

### Page 5: Nexa Paraguay vs Competitors

- **Price:** 2,900-6,900 (fixed) vs 3,000-10,000+ (variable)
- **Trips required:** 1 (2-3 days) vs 1-3
- **Timeline:** 8-12 weeks vs 12-24 weeks
- **Banking:** Included introduction vs self-serve
- **Languages:** EN/ES/NL/DE vs usually only ES/EN

## CTA Segmentation

| Audience   | CTA Message |
|------------|-------------|
| Dutch      | "Calculate your specific savings" |
| Belgian    | "What's your box 2 / exit tax situation?" |
| German     | "Exit tax from Germany works differently. Let's talk." |
| General    | "Book a free 30-minute call" |
"}

{"path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/06-marketing/email-sequences.md", "content": "# Email Sequences — Automated Nurture & Post-Consultation

**Purpose:** Defines the 2 primary automated email sequences (A: Tax-Driven
Interest, B: Post-Consultation) plus the 7-email nurture JSON used by Mailchimp.

**Last updated:** 2026-04

**Cross-references:** `lead-magnets.md`, `faq-dealclosing.md`,
`/root/nexa-paraguay/email-nurture.json`, integration guide (Mailchimp setup)

---

## Overview

Rather than 4 separate sequences, we consolidate to 2 high-impact sequences
that cover 90% of use cases. All content is multi-locale (EN/ES/NL/DE).

## Sequence A: Tax-Driven Interest

**Trigger:** Form submission or lead magnet download
**Duration:** 5 emails over 14 days

| #  | Day | Subject (EN) | Focus |
|----|-----|-------------|-------|
| 1  | 0   | "Your Paraguay tax analysis is ready" | Welcome, intro to territorial system |
| 2  | 3   | "What you could save — real numbers" | Tax comparison table (100K income) |
| 3  | 7   | "8-12 weeks. One trip. Here's how." | Process walkthrough |
| 4  | 10  | "What clients say after 12 months" | Social proof, testimonials |
| 5  | 14  | "Let's talk — no pressure" | Summary + final CTA |

**Tags:** Sequence A triggered by `nexa-lead` tag.

## Sequence B: Post-Consultation

**Trigger:** Consultation booking
**Duration:** 4 emails over 10 days

| #  | Day | Subject (EN) | Focus |
|----|-----|-------------|-------|
| 1  | 0   | "Thanks for the call — here's your summary" | Recap + next steps |
| 2  | 2   | "Your personalized Paraguay plan" | Tailored tax/savings breakdown |
| 3  | 5   | "Getting started — document checklist" | Required docs + timeline |
| 4  | 10  | "Still the right fit?" | Final nudge, urgency |

**Tags:** Sequence B triggered by `nexa-consultation` tag.

## Mailchimp Nurture JSON (7-Email)

File: `/root/nexa-paraguay/email-nurture.json`

A 7-email alternative nurture sequence (35-day span) covering:

| Day | Subject (EN) |
|-----|-------------|
| 0   | "Thank you for your interest in Paraguay" |
| 3   | "What makes Paraguay different" |
| 7   | "Our process, step by step" |
| 12  | "Everything in one trip — how it works" |
| 18  | "What no one tells you about banking in Paraguay" |
| 25  | "Which program is right for you?" |
| 35  | "Ready for the next step?" |

All emails are 4-locale (nl/en/de/es). Import into Mailchimp as a Customer
Journey triggered by tag `nexa-paraguay-lead`.

## Personalization Variables

```
{{firstName}}    — from lead data
{{senderName}}   — assigned account manager
{{program}}      — Base/Business/Investor
{{savings}}      — calculated from income input
```
"}

{"path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/06-marketing/faq-dealclosing.md", "content": "# FAQ — Top 15 Deal-Closing Questions

**Purpose:** Defensive and objection-handling content for the FAQ section and
sales conversations. Each answer connects back to the 10% tax story.

**Last updated:** 2026-04

**Cross-references:** `comparisons.md`, `email-sequences.md`,
`/root/nexa-paraguay/marketing/faq-dealclosing.md` (source)

---

## Tax Questions (Core)

| #  | Question | Short Answer |
|----|----------|--------------|
| 1  | Is the 10% rate guaranteed? | Yes — Ley [REDACTED_PHONE]), stable since 2014 |
| 2  | Will my home country still tax me? | No — once you properly relocate |
| 3  | What about Dutch exit tax / box 3? | Can be deferred, not avoided — we coordinate |
| 4  | How does this compare to Portugal's NHR? | NHR is dead ([REDACTED_PHONE]). Paraguay wins. |
| 5  | Is Paraguay on the EU blacklist? | No. Not blacklisted. CRS-participating. |

**Detail:** The 10% IRE is the standard corporate rate, not a loophole.
Territorial system means foreign-sourced income (dividends, royalties,
consulting) is taxed at 0%. EU/CRS exchange means accounts are visible but
tax residency, not account location, determines liability.

## Process Questions

| #  | Question | Short Answer |
|----|----------|--------------|
| 6  | How long does it take? | 8-12 weeks from document submission |
| 7  | How many trips? | One (2-3 days in Asuncion) |
| 8  | What documents? | Passport, birth cert, marriage cert, bank stmts, background check |
| 9  | What if rejected? | 97% completion rate; we pre-screen thoroughly |

## Financial Questions

| #  | Question | Short Answer |
|----|----------|--------------|
| 10 | How much does it cost? | 2,900-6,900 fixed depending on program |
| 11 | What about banking? | We introduce you to Banco do Brasil, Continental, Itau |
| 12 | Can I keep my home-country account? | Yes — keep 6-12 months for clean transition |

## Lifestyle Questions

| #  | Question | Short Answer |
|----|----------|--------------|
| 13 | Is it safe? | Expat neighborhoods safer than many assume |
| 14 | What about schools? | American School, Colegium, British School available |
| 15 | Can I work remotely? | Yes — structure as self-employed or via Paraguayan company |

## Pricing by Program

| Program | Price | Includes |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Base    | 2,900 | Residency + docs + process coordination |
| Business| 4,400+ | Above + company setup + RUC + bank + accounting |
| Investor| 6,900+ | Above + SUACE + permanent path + extended advisory |

All fixed prices include legal fees, notary, government taxes, bank
coordination, and 12 months advisory. No surprise fees.
"}

{"path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/06-marketing/lead-magnets.md", "content": "# Lead Magnets — Capture & Convert

**Purpose:** Specifies the 4 lead magnets, their user flow, implementation
details, and tracking schema used to capture email leads for the nurture
sequence.

**Last updated:** 2026-04

**Cross-references:** `email-sequences.md`, `faq-dealclosing.md`,
`/root/nexa-paraguay/marketing/lead-magnets.md` (source)

---

## Strategy

Lead with the tax calculator. It is interactive, personalizable, and delivers
immediate value. Everything else is secondary.

## Lead Magnet 1: Tax Savings Calculator (Primary)

- **Name:** "Paraguay Tax Savings Calculator"
- **Format:** Interactive web tool + PDF report (email-gated)
- **Capture:** Email required for full PDF
- **CTA flow:** Calculate My Savings -> Book Consultation

**User flow:**
1. Landing: "See how much you could save with Paraguay's 10% IRE"
2. Inputs: Country, annual income (20K-500K+), income type, shares?, foreign income?
3. Real-time comparison: current tax vs potential, annual + 5-year savings
4. PDF report delivered by email triggers Sequence A

**PDF sections:** Tax comparison, math explanation, process overview, next steps.

## Lead Magnet 2: Tax Guide PDF (Secondary)

- **Name:** "2026 Paraguay Tax Guide: Everything You Need to Know"
- **Format:** 12-15 page PDF
- **Chapters:** IRE explained, territorial system, country comparisons, exit tax, banking, process, FAQs

## Lead Magnet 3: Residency Document Checklist (Tertiary)

- **Format:** Interactive checklist (Notion-style or PDF)
- **Email capture:** Optional

## Lead Magnet 4: First 30 Days Guide (Post-Arrival)

- **Format:** PDF checklist
- **No email capture** (free for clients, post-arrival upsell)

## Implementation Priority

| Rank | Magnet | Priority | Timeline |
|------|--------|----------|----------|
| 1    | Tax Calculator | HIGH | Week 1 |
| 2    | Tax Guide PDF | HIGH | Week 2 |
| 3    | Document Checklist | Medium | Week 3 |
| 4    | First 30 Days | Low | Week 4 |

## Tracking Events

| Event | Where | Purpose |
|-------|-------|---------|
| `lead_magnet_view` | Landing page | Top of funnel |
| `lead_magnet_start` | Calculator started | Intent signal |
| `lead_magnet_complete` | Calculation done | High intent |
| `lead_magnet_pdf_download` | PDF requested | Email capture |
| `lead_magnet_cta_click` | "Book Consultation" | Conversion |

## Supabase Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE lead_magnet_leads (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  email TEXT, first_name TEXT, country_of_residence TEXT,
  annual_income_estimate TEXT, income_type TEXT,
  has_company_shares BOOLEAN, has_foreign_income BOOLEAN,
  calculated_savings DECIMAL, source TEXT DEFAULT 'tax_calculator',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
```
"}

DeepSeek AI with per-client memory (LightRAG) and business tools.

Evolution API instance, seeding LightRAG with FAQ/pricing, and configuring
AI mode (ventas/soporte/default).

See `/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/08-integrations/whatsapp-ai-bridge.md` for
detailed integration status.

## Overview

An AI agent hosted at **whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud** is designed to handle
WhatsApp conversations for Nexa Paraguay. It uses:
- **DeepSeek AI** for natural language processing
- **LightRAG** for per-client conversation memory
- **Evolution API** instances for WhatsApp message relay

## What the AI Can Handle

| Area | Examples |
|------|----------|
| Visa questions | Requirements, timeline, cost |
| Service info | Program comparison, pricing |
| Document requirements | List of needed docs, apostille |
| FAQs | Tax questions, safety, schools |
| Scheduling | Booking consultations |

## Integration Steps (Pending)

2. **Seed LightRAG** with: product catalog, FAQ, pricing, policies
3. **Configure AI mode** — `ventas` (sales), `soporte` (support), or `default`
4. **Replace static WhatsApp number** on the website with the AI-connected number

## API Reference

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| /clients | POST | Create a new client instance |
| /clients | GET | List existing clients |
| /health | GET | Health check |

Full documentation: `/root/paragu-ai-builder/docs/whatsapp-ai-integration.md`

## Current Status

- **WhatsApp Business account:** Set up ([REDACTED_PHONE])
- **Click-to-chat float:** Implemented on website
- **AI Agent service:** Available at whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud
- **Connection to live number:** NOT yet established
- **LightRAG seeding:** NOT yet done
- **AI mode:** Not yet configured

## Next Steps

1. Provision an Evolution API instance for the Nexa number
2. Upload the FAQ, pricing page, and program docs as RAG seed data
3. Set AI mode to `ventas` for automated qualification and booking
4. Test with a sandbox number before switching live
"}
